title: State of Arabia Before Islam
story: Arabia was fragmented into tribes with no central authority. Idol worship dominated religious life, with over 360 idols placed in the Kaaba. Social injustice was widespread, including oppression of the weak and the practice of burying daughters alive.
narration: Before the rise of Islam… Arabia stood divided… tribes fought endlessly, and truth had been replaced with tradition and power…

title: Lineage of Prophet Muhammad ﷺ
story: The Prophet ﷺ belonged to the noble Quraysh tribe, specifically Banu Hashim, known for their leadership and honor among Arabs.
narration: From a noble lineage… a man would rise… not with power… but with truth…

title: Birth of the Prophet ﷺ
story: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ was born in 570 CE, the Year of the Elephant. His father had passed away before his birth, leaving him an orphan.
narration: In a year marked by عظيمة events… a child was born… who would change the course of history…
lessons: Great beginnings often come through hardship

title: Childhood in the Desert
story: He was sent to live with Halima Saadia in the desert, where he grew in strength and purity of language. Blessings increased in her household.
narration: In the quiet desert… far from the noise of the city… he grew… under divine care…

title: Chest Opening Incident
story: During childhood, angels opened his chest and purified his heart, symbolizing divine preparation for his future mission.
narration: Even as a child… he was being prepared… for a mission beyond human understanding…

title: Loss of Mother and Grandfather
story: His mother passed away when he was six, followed by his grandfather. He was then raised by his uncle Abu Talib.
narration: Loss followed loss… yet strength continued to grow…

title: Youth and Character
story: He worked as a shepherd and later as a trader. His honesty earned him the title Al-Ameen.
narration: In a world of deception… he became known as the truthful one…

title: Hilf al-Fudul
story: He participated in an alliance to uphold justice in Makkah, showing his commitment to fairness even before Prophethood.
narration: Before revelation… he stood for justice…

title: Marriage to Khadijah
story: Khadijah (RA), impressed by his honesty, proposed marriage. Their marriage provided emotional and financial stability.
narration: In Khadijah… he found support… belief… and peace…

title: Rebuilding the Kaaba
story: He resolved a dispute among tribes over placing the Black Stone by suggesting a collective solution.
narration: A conflict… resolved with wisdom…

title: First Revelation
story: At age 40, in Cave Hira, Angel Jibreel commanded him to read, marking the beginning of Prophethood.
narration: In silence… revelation descended… and history changed forever…

title: Early Converts
story: The first believers included Khadijah, Abu Bakr, Ali, and Zaid.
narration: The message began… quietly… in hearts…

title: Secret Preaching
story: For three years, Islam spread privately among trusted individuals.
narration: Not with noise… but with sincerity… the message spread…

title: Public Call at Mount Safa
story: The Prophet ﷺ publicly دعوت Quraysh, warning them of accountability.
narration: The silence ended… the message was now for all…

title: Persecution Begins
story: Muslims were tortured and oppressed. Sumayyah became the first martyr.
narration: Faith was tested… not in ease… but in pain…

title: Migration to Abyssinia
story: Muslims migrated to Abyssinia seeking justice under a fair Christian king.
narration: When الأرض became tight… they sought justice elsewhere…

title: Boycott of Banu Hashim
story: Quraysh imposed a harsh boycott isolating Muslims socially and economically.
narration: For years… hunger replaced comfort… but faith remained…

title: Year of Sorrow
story: Khadijah and Abu Talib passed away, leaving the Prophet ﷺ without key support.
narration: The pillars of support were gone… but the mission remained…
key_events: Deaths of Khadijah and Abu Talib

title: Journey to Taif
story: He traveled to Taif seeking support but was rejected and attacked.
narration: Bleeding… yet praying… for their guidance…

title: Isra and Mi'raj
story: The miraculous night journey strengthened faith and established prayer.
narration: From earth… to the heavens… in one night…

title: Pledges of Aqabah
story: People from Yathrib (Madinah) secretly met the Prophet ﷺ and pledged allegiance, promising to protect him if he migrated.
narration: In the darkness of night… بعيد from the eyes of Quraysh… a promise was made… one that would change history…
key_events: First and Second Pledge of Aqabah
lessons: Support can arise from unexpected places

title: Permission to Migrate
story: Muslims were given permission to migrate to Madinah as persecution intensified.
narration: The الأرض had become too tight… but a new horizon was opening…
lessons: Sacrifice for faith leads to new beginnings

title: Quraysh Plot Assassination
story: Quraysh leaders planned to قتل the Prophet ﷺ by selecting شباب from each tribe.
narration: They thought they could silence the truth… but truth cannot be killed…

title: Night of Escape
story: Ali (RA) slept in the Prophet’s bed while he secretly left with Abu Bakr.
narration: Under the cover of night… courage replaced fear…
lessons: Trust and sacrifice go hand in hand

title: Cave of Thawr
story: They hid in Cave Thawr for three days while Quraysh searched for them.
narration: Just steps away… yet unseen… divine protection surrounded them…
lessons: Allah protects those who trust Him

title: Journey to Madinah
story: The journey to Madinah was filled with danger, but they reached safely.
narration: Across the desert… with danger at every step… yet guided by faith…

title: Arrival at Quba
story: The Prophet ﷺ arrived in Quba and built the first mosque.
narration: The first foundation… of a new society… was laid…

title: Arrival in Madinah
story: The people of Madinah welcomed the Prophet ﷺ with joy and unity.
narration: A city once divided… now united in hope…

title: Building Masjid an-Nabawi
story: The mosque became the center of community, governance, and worship.
narration: Not just a mosque… but the heart of a civilization…

title: Brotherhood (Mu’akhah)
story: The Prophet ﷺ established brotherhood between Muhajirun and Ansar.
narration: They were no longer strangers… they were brothers…

title: Constitution of Madinah
story: A formal agreement ensured justice and coexistence among all groups.
narration: For the first time… law replaced tribal chaos…

title: First Expeditions
story: Small expeditions were sent to monitor Quraysh activities.
narration: Preparation began… not for conquest… but for survival…

title: Battle of Badr Begins
story: Muslims set out to intercept a caravan but faced Quraysh army.
narration: A small group… facing a powerful army…

title: Victory at Badr
story: Despite being outnumbered, Muslims achieved victory with divine help.
narration: Numbers did not decide… faith did…

title: Aftermath of Badr
story: Prisoners were treated with mercy and justice.
narration: Even in victory… mercy remained…

title: Battle of Uhud Begins
story: Quraysh sought revenge and marched towards Madinah.
narration: The desire for revenge… led them back…

title: Turning Point at Uhud
story: Archers left their positions, leading to a surprise attack.
narration: A single decision… changed the outcome…

title: Aftermath of Uhud
story: The Prophet ﷺ was injured, and many companions were martyred.
narration: Pain… loss… but not defeat…

title: Expulsion of Banu Nadir
story: A Jewish tribe violated agreements and was expelled.
narration: Trust was broken… and justice followed…

title: Battle of Khandaq
story: A trench was dug to defend Madinah against a large coalition.
narration: Not by القوة… but by strategy… they defended…

title: Siege of Madinah
story: Coalition forces surrounded Madinah but failed to break defenses.
narration: They surrounded the city… but could not break its resolve…

title: Collapse of Coalition
story: Harsh winds and اختلاف weakened the enemy forces.
narration: Without a battle… victory came…

title: Banu Qurayzah Incident
story: Another tribe betrayed agreements during the siege and faced consequences.
narration: In times of crisis… loyalty is tested…

title: Stability After Khandaq
story: The failure of the coalition marked a turning point in strength.
narration: The tide had turned… the قوة of opposition weakened…

title: Preparation for Umrah
story: The Prophet ﷺ set out peacefully to perform Umrah.
narration: Not for war… but for worship… they set out…

title: Stopped at Hudaybiyyah
story: Quraysh blocked entry, leading to negotiations.
narration: At the edge of Makkah… a pause… that would shape history…

title: Treaty of Hudaybiyyah
story: A peace treaty was signed, appearing unfavorable but strategically beneficial.
narration: What seemed like loss… was actually a hidden victory…
lessons: Short-term sacrifice leads to long-term success

title: Return to Madinah
story: Muslims returned without Umrah but with peace secured.
narration: They returned… not defeated… but stronger…

title: Spread of Islam Accelerates
story: With peace, many embraced Islam rapidly.
narration: With barriers removed… hearts opened…

title: Letters to Kings
story: The Prophet ﷺ sent messages inviting global leaders to Islam.
narration: The message was no longer regional… it was for the world…

title: Violation of Hudaybiyyah Treaty
story: Allied tribes of Quraysh violated the treaty by attacking a Muslim-allied tribe, breaking the peace agreement.
narration: The peace that was meant to last… was broken… and with it… the path to a decisive moment opened…

title: Preparation for Conquest of Makkah
story: The Prophet ﷺ prepared a large army of 10,000 to march toward Makkah.
narration: Silently… strategically… a force was gathering… not for revenge… but for resolution…

title: March to Makkah
story: The Muslim army advanced toward Makkah with minimal resistance.
narration: Step by step… the المدينة they once left… came closer again…

title: Conquest of Makkah
story: Makkah was conquered peacefully, marking a turning point in Islamic history.
narration: The city that once rejected him… now stood open… not conquered by force… but by truth…

title: Destruction of Idols
story: The Prophet ﷺ cleansed the Kaaba of idols, restoring pure monotheism.
narration: Falsehood fell… one idol at a time… truth stood alone…

title: General Amnesty
story: The Prophet ﷺ forgave the people of Makkah despite years of persecution.
narration: No revenge… no anger… only mercy… ‘You are all free.’

title: Battle of Hunayn
story: Muslims faced a surprise attack but regrouped and achieved victory.
narration: Confidence shook… but faith restored the ground beneath them…

title: Siege of Taif
story: After Hunayn, Muslims laid siege to Taif but later withdrew.
narration: Not every victory comes immediately… patience completes what effort begins…

title: Delegations Arrive
story: Tribes from across Arabia came to accept Islam.
narration: One by one… tribes arrived… not in fear… but in belief…

title: Expedition of Tabuk
story: A difficult journey north tested commitment, though no battle occurred.
narration: Not every test is a battle… some are journeys of endurance…

title: Hypocrites Exposed
story: Some stayed behind during Tabuk, revealing hypocrisy.
narration: In ease… all claim loyalty… but hardship reveals truth…

title: Kaaba Purified Completely
story: Islamic monotheism was firmly established in Makkah.
narration: The house built for one God… returned to its purpose…

title: Farewell Pilgrimage
story: The Prophet ﷺ performed Hajj, demonstrating rituals and unity.
narration: A final gathering… a complete message…

title: Farewell Sermon
story: He delivered a powerful message emphasizing equality and justice.
narration: No Arab over non-Arab… no superiority… except in righteousness…

title: Completion of Religion
story: The revelation confirmed the completion of Islam.
narration: Today… your religion is complete…

title: Final Illness
story: The Prophet ﷺ fell ill and gave final instructions.
narration: Even in illness… the mission continued…

title: Last Advice
story: He emphasized prayer and care for others.
narration: Hold onto prayer… and treat others well…

title: Passing of the Prophet ﷺ
story: He passed away in Madinah, leaving behind a complete message.
narration: The mission ended… but the message remained…

title: Reaction of Companions
story: Companions were shocked until Abu Bakr reminded them of reality.
narration: Whoever worshipped Muhammad… he has passed… but Allah lives forever…

title: Legacy of Islam
story: Islam spread beyond Arabia, carrying the message forward.
narration: From a single city… to the world… the message continued…

title: Character of the Prophet ﷺ
story: His life reflected mercy, patience, justice, and leadership.
narration: He was not just a leader… he was a model…

title: Impact on Humanity
story: His message transformed societies and continues to guide billions.
narration: From darkness… to light… a transformation unmatched…

title: Completion of Mission
story: The mission of delivering the message was fully accomplished.
narration: Nothing was left incomplete…

title: Lessons from Seerah
story: The Seerah provides lessons in leadership, patience, and faith.
narration: Every moment… carries a lesson… for every generation…

title: End of Seerah
story: The life of the Prophet ﷺ remains a timeless guide for humanity.
narration: The story ends… but its guidance never does…
